---
title = "Project Structure"
---

# Simple rendering

For a document with default settings, just render it:

```
calepin render paper.qmd
```

No configuration, no scaffolding. *Calepin* uses built-in templates and writes the output alongside the input file.

# Customizing a document

To customize the appearance of a single document, scaffold a sidecar directory:

```
calepin init sidecar paper.qmd --partials
```

This creates a `paper_calepin/` directory next to your file, containing a `config.toml` and all built-in partials (templates). Edit the partials to change how your document looks, then render as usual:

```
calepin render paper.qmd
```

The sidecar directory structure:

```
paper.qmd
paper_calepin/
  config.toml            # per-document configuration
  partials/              # template overrides
    html/
      page.html
      figure.html
    latex/
      page.tex
  cache/                 # code chunk cache
  files/                 # generated figures
```

When a sidecar `partials/` directory exists, *Calepin* uses only those templates. Built-in templates are ignored entirely. This gives you full control over every piece of output.

# Websites

To scaffold a website:

```
calepin init website my_site
```

This creates a directory with a `_calepin/config.toml` manifest, an `index.qmd`, and example pages:

```
my_site/
  _calepin/
    config.toml          # project configuration
    partials/            # template overrides
      website/
        page.html
        navbar.html
      html/
        figure.html
    assets/              # CSS, images, logos
  index.qmd
  about.qmd
  guide/
    intro.qmd
    advanced.qmd
  _calepin_output/       # generated output (should be in .gitignore)
```

Preview immediately:

```
calepin preview my_site
```

# Key directories

| Path | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `_calepin/config.toml` | Project configuration (targets, sidebar, navbar, etc.) |
| `_calepin/partials/` | Template overrides. Drop a file here to replace any built-in template. |
| `_calepin/assets/` | Static assets (CSS, images, logos) copied to output. |
| `{stem}_calepin/` | Per-document sidecar: configuration, cached code output, generated figures. |
| `_calepin_output/` | Default output directory for websites (configurable in `config.toml`). |

# Scaffolding commands

```
calepin init sidecar paper.qmd     # sidecar for a single document
calepin init notebook my-paper     # notebook project
calepin init website my-site       # website project
calepin init book my-book          # book project
calepin init partials              # update local partials to latest built-in templates
```

## Updating partials

When *Calepin* is updated, the built-in templates may change (new features, bug fixes, `link()` support). If your project has custom partials in `_calepin/partials/`, they won't pick up these changes automatically.

Run `calepin init partials` from your project root to overwrite all local partials that have a matching built-in template. The command lists the number of files and asks for confirmation before writing.

::: {.callout-warning}
This is destructive: any customizations you made to local partials will be lost. Commit or back up your partials before running this command.
:::
